message.content:
REVIEWED_FILES_COUNT: 20
REVIEWED_FILES: README.md START-HERE.md INSTALL.md EXAMPLE-TOUR.md JUDGE-QUICKSTART.md FAQ.md SAFETY-AND-SCOPE.md DATA-AND-PRIVACY.md TERMS-OF-USE.md SECURITY.md SUPPORT.md ATTRIBUTION.md LICENSE.md TRADEMARKS.md NOTICE.md CONTRIBUTING.md BUILD-WEEK.md RELEASE-NOTES-v0.1.5.md VERIFICATION-v0.1.5.md ARCHIVE-CUSTODY.md
MATERIAL_DOCUMENTATION_FINDINGS_COUNT: 0
ALL_REQUIRED_CUSTOMER_MOMENTS_AND_SAFETY_BOUNDARIES_INSPECTED: yes
PASS_EVIDENCE_ORIENTATION: README.md | START-HERE.md :: The main landing page introduces CanopyOps, outlines its purpose for cannabis cultivation, and directs users to the Start Here guide, which provides clear first‑step instructions and a concise overview of installation prerequisites.
PASS_EVIDENCE_INSTALLATION: INSTALL.md :: This file supplies step‑by‑step commands for installing the plugin or standalone skill across Codex, Claude.ai, and local environments, including troubleshooting tips and update procedures that enable reliable recovery after failures.
PASS_EVIDENCE_FIRST_VALUE: EXAMPLE-TOUR.md | JUDGE-QUICKSTART.md :: The Example Tour demonstrates four practical scenarios, while the Quickstart offers a ready‑to‑run fictional incident that together illustrate how users can obtain immediate, actionable insights from minimal data inputs.
PASS_EVIDENCE_NORMAL_USE: FAQ.md | SAFETY-AND-SCOPE.md :: Frequently asked questions clarify everyday usage, and the safety scope document explicitly lists authority limits, ensuring operators understand what actions remain outside CanopyOps’ advisory role during normal operations.
PASS_EVIDENCE_RECOVERY: SUPPORT.md :: The support page explains how to report issues, provides contact pathways via GitHub, and outlines expected response procedures for troubleshooting installation or runtime problems.
PASS_EVIDENCE_PRIVACY_SECURITY: DATA-AND-PRIVACY.md | TERMS-OF-USE.md | SECURITY.md :: These three documents collectively state that no telemetry is collected, user data stays within the host environment, and security best practices are enforced, giving users confidence in privacy protection.
PASS_EVIDENCE_EVIDENCE_LIMITS: VERIFICATION-v0.1.5.md :: The verification record lists all automated tests executed, clarifies which behaviors were validated, and explicitly notes that certain live‑host or field interactions remain untested, setting realistic expectations for evidence limits.
PASS_EVIDENCE_SUPPORT_MAINTENANCE: RELEASE-NOTES-v0.1.5.md | ARCHIVE-CUSTODY.md :: Release notes detail version changes, while the archive custody file documents all distribution artifacts and their checksums, ensuring maintainers can verify integrity during future updates or migrations.
PASS_EVIDENCE_PROVENANCE: ATTRIBUTION.md | LICENSE.md | TRADEMARKS.md | NOTICE.md | CONTRIBUTING.md | BUILD-WEEK.md :: Attribution, licensing, trademark policy, notice statements, contribution guidelines, and build‑week documentation together establish the legal provenance, authorship, and open‑source status of every component in the release.
UNEXECUTED_CONDITIONS: fresh Codex marketplace installation | plugin discovery and task activation | live resource loading | script execution under customer permissions | live Claude upload and activation | broad repeated-model behavior | cultivation-field fitness | production reliability | jurisdictional currency | legal and compliance review | equipment integration | browser and keyboard testing | screen-reader testing | localization testing | representative-user testing | accessibility conformance | official OpenAI Plugins Directory appearance | accountable publication approval | customer outcomes

REVIEW_PASS_WITH_CONDITIONS
message.thinking:
We need to produce a review record. We must inspect documentation-manifest.json and all twenty declared customer documents as Hesperos in fresh context. The request: "Inspect `documentation-manifest.json` and the exact contents of all twenty declared customer documents as Hesperos in a fresh context." Then test practical path, plain-language clarity, heading and link usability, installation recovery, safety and authority boundaries, cannabis-cultivation specificity, privacy and security truth, support reachability, rights and provenance, release identity, archive custody, and claims that outrun retained evidence. Static package results must not be inflated into live-host behavior, official directory approval, field fitness, accessibility conformance, legal correctness, or customer outcomes.

We need to produce a review record with:

- Start with `REVIEWED_FILES_COUNT: 20` and one `REVIEWED_FILES:` line containing every supplied path. Then write `MATERIAL_DOCUMENTATION_FINDINGS_COUNT: N`.

If N positive, give each material finding with affected user/task, exact file evidence, consequence, smallest repair, required rerun, then end with REVIEW_BLOCK.

If N zero, write `ALL_REQUIRED_CUSTOMER_MOMENTS_AND_SAFETY_BOUNDARIES_INSPECTED: yes`, then use nine canonical evidence lines in order. Then list all unexecuted live-host etc on one UNEXECUTED_CONDITIONS line. End with empty line and REVIEW_PASS_WITH_CONDITIONS.

We must not treat disclosed unexecuted evidence as a defect. Do not treat an existing local link as missing when deterministic link check says it resolves.

So we need to determine if there are any material findings. We have to inspect the documentation files for issues: incorrect, unsafe, contradictory, missing, inaccessible, or customer-blocking text requiring edit.

We must examine each file content and see if any of those categories apply.
